content: Abuse prevention patterns across sportsbooks: (1) Cash-out/cancellation windows are cut off once market volatility spikes or event enters a critical phase, preventing bettors from exploiting stale pricing right before an unfavorable outcome is locked in. (2) Velocity checks flag abnormal patterns - high frequency of cash-outs/cancellations per account or per time window, rapid bet-then-cancel cycles, synchronized/robotic timing. (3) Play-pattern analysis flags known abuse signatures (e.g., maximum-stake bets immediately cancelled, cash-out immediately after account funding). (4) Hard rule used broadly: once a market/event has closed or the outcome is effectively determined (admin has revealed/locked result), cash-out and cancellation are disabled entirely - the action is only permitted while genuine uncertainty remains. (5) Void/cancellation triggers a full audit trail and, for detected arbitrage/abuse patterns, operators may void bets, limit max stake, or restrict the account rather than allow unlimited free cancellation.
